title: DiagnoseResult
description: Output of cli-agent-diagnose: matched §N failure modes with workarounds

schema_version.description: Schema version for adapter compatibility checks

matches.description: Matched failure modes, sorted by confidence descending

no_match.description: True when the trace has output but no failure mode matched above the threshold

trace_insufficient.description: True when stdout and stderr are too sparse to classify; check suggested_context

suggested_context.description: Hints for what additional trace data would enable classification; non-empty only when trace_insufficient is true

trace_summary.description: One-line human-readable summary of the parsed trace (commands seen, failure count, retry count)

failure_mode_id.description: §N identifier from the CLI Agent Spec failure taxonomy

title.description: Human-readable failure mode title from the challenge file

confidence.description: Classification confidence; ≥0.80 from deterministic matching, LLM-adjusted otherwise

evidence.description: One sentence explaining what in the trace triggered this match

workaround.description: Full text of the ### Agent Workaround section from the challenge file

memory.description: One-sentence fact to store in agent memory; prevents repeating this failure in future sessions

skill_patch.description: Reusable rule to add to the agent system prompt or skill file; generalises beyond this specific call

severity.description: Severity from the challenge file frontmatter

spec_link.description: Relative path to the challenge file from the repo root

limitation.description: **Limitation:** line from the Agent Workaround section; empty string if none

source.description: Whether the match came from deterministic signal matching or LLM classification
